summary: Iteration 5 — Frontend-only verification of Microsoft 365 admin iframe-redirect fix. All critical flows pass: page renders, redirect URI card + Copy button work, Connect button correctly gates on setupReady, full-page redirect to login.microsoftonline.com fires with the correct OAuth params, and the Microsoft Sign-in page actually loads (confirming X-Frame-Options is bypassed via window.top.location.href).

integration_issues.issue: The redirect_uri returned by GET /api/integrations/microsoft365/redirect-uri and displayed in the admin card uses origin 'https://3dd7823d-4048-456d-b31d-e4cb598dc869.preview.emergentagent.com' while frontend's REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is 'https://whs-compliance.preview.emergentagent.com'. The path suffix '/api/integrations/microsoft365/oauth/callback' is correct, but the origin does NOT match REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL as the spec required. Azure will need to register the backend-emitted URI exactly, so it functionally works, but the spec stated the URI must 'start with the same origin as REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL'. Likely backend BACKEND_URL/PUBLIC_BASE_URL env points to a different ingress hostname.

action_items: Optional: reconcile backend BACKEND_URL (used to build redirect_uri) with frontend REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL so both reflect the same public origin. Functionally the OAuth flow still navigates correctly with the backend-emitted URI, but the spec said the displayed URI must share REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL's origin.
critical_code_review_comments: Microsoft365Admin.jsx uses (window.top || window).location.href = data.authorize_url — correct iframe-busting pattern, verified by Microsoft sign-in page actually loading.
setupReady gating correctly checks all 4 fields including secretOnFile OR secretInput, so an already-saved secret still counts as ready.
Copy handler gracefully falls back when navigator.clipboard fails (toast.error).
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing test config saved from previous iteration; re-saved tenant_id='common', client_id='00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000001234', a fresh client_secret, sender_email='hseq@paneltec.com.au'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 confirmed: (1) redirect URI card shows URI ending in '/api/integrations/microsoft365/oauth/callback' with working Copy button + 'Redirect URI copied' toast; (2) Connect button background is rgb(44,107,255)=#2C6BFF and enabled when setupReady; (3) clicking Connect performs a full-page redirect to https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?... containing client_id, redirect_uri, response_type, response_mode, scope (offline_access+Mail.Send+User.Read), state, prompt — screenshot proves Microsoft Sign-in page actually rendered. The grey/disabled state (#94A3B8) for !setupReady was NOT directly observed in browser because previous iteration left all 4 fields saved (per agent-to-agent note); code review confirms the logic is correctly wired (style backgroundColor uses ternary on setupReady; data-testid='m365-setup-missing' renders only when !setupReady). Minor discrepancy: displayed redirect_uri origin differs from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — see integration_issues.

rca of the issue.original_bug: Clicking Connect to Microsoft showed 'login.microsoftonline.com refused to connect' because the app's React shell embeds pages in an iframe and Microsoft sets X-Frame-Options: DENY on its login page.
rca of the issue.fix_applied: Microsoft365Admin.jsx connect() now does `(window.top || window).location.href = data.authorize_url` to perform a full-page navigation that breaks out of the iframe shell. Setup gating added so the button is grey/disabled until all 4 fields are saved. Redirect URI surfaced with Copy button via GET /api/integrations/microsoft365/redirect-uri.
rca of the issue.verification: After clicking Connect, page.url() became 'https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?...' and the Microsoft Sign-in form actually rendered (screenshot), confirming the iframe-bust succeeded and Microsoft did NOT refuse the connection.
